name: BTC Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: scale-in around value recovery when confirmation improves across bars instead of one-shot all-in timing
thesis: BTC 4h is the cleanest primary lane for testing whether value recovery should be accumulated across improving confirmation rather than expressed as a one-bar all-in reclaim. In a chop-heavy environment, gradual confirmation may capture repaired balance moves without depending on a single perfect timing bar.
invalidation_condition: Abort if value recovery still lacks trade density, if staggered confirmation never improves economics after costs, or if scale-in simply adds complexity without changing outcomes.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price returns toward value after a flush, confirms recovery over more than one bar, and then moves far enough toward balance for staged participation to help.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Choppy markets often repair in steps rather than one decisive reclaim bar. Scaling in as confirmation improves may match that repair path better than a single-entry expression.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is the best fit because recovery structure is readable and less noisy than on thinner assets, while still offering enough amplitude for staged entries.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market only mean-reverts shallowly and never develops a cleaner recovery sequence, scale-in will add complexity without improving expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the recovery thesis is correct but the second scale-in is unnecessary, simplify to one entry with the same confirmation stack before changing the core recovery idea.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A limited but usable number of recovery trades where the second entry only appears when the recovery actually stabilizes.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 4h and one cross-lane show credible density plus better post-cost recovery behavior than one-shot reclaim logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse participation, or no measurable benefit from staged confirmation.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; recovery and improving confirmation are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad recovery logic rather than rare capitulation-only triggers

validation_basket.why_included: Best control lane for staged value-recovery participation.
validation_basket.continue_if: Recovery trades show enough density and improved economics from staged confirmation.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The setup is too sparse or staged entry adds no benefit.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Flushes recover in steps and the second entry improves average position quality.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism transfers to the similar-asset lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows the same stepwise recovery behavior.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly noisier but still tradable staged recoveries.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same recovery logic survives on a faster adjacent lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: 1h preserves the mechanism without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The faster lane overtrades without edge.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More signals than 4h with smaller but still tradable recovery legs.

long: Entry tranche 2: Next bar Close >= prior Close
short: Entry tranche 2: Next bar Close <= prior Close

long: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 below average entry
short: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 above average entry
